popularity: very_high — most common CRM dual-use setup (HubSpot for Marketing, Salesforce for Sales)

authentication_requirements.hubspot_side: OAuth 2.0 or Private App Token with scopes for contacts, deals, companies, activities
authentication_requirements.salesforce_side: OAuth 2.0 JWT Bearer or Web Server flow. Integration user must have API Enabled permission, Read/Edit on all synced objects. Dedicated integration user recommended.

common_use_cases: Lead handoff from HubSpot Marketing to Salesforce Sales — MQLs become Leads/Opportunities in Salesforce
Bi-directional contact sync for unified customer view
Deal stage sync — Salesforce opportunity stages update HubSpot deal stages and vice versa
Activity sync — HubSpot calls, meetings, emails logged in Salesforce
Campaign member sync — HubSpot email campaign responses create/update Salesforce Campaign Members

hubspot_endpoints.purpose: Search contacts by email or custom property

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: HubSpot sends a picklist value that doesn't exist in Salesforce picklist (or vice versa). Salesforce 'Restrict picklist' setting is ON.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Turn OFF 'Restrict picklist to values defined in value set' in Salesforce field settings. Or ensure HubSpot values match Salesforce picklist options exactly. Audit all picklist mappings before going live.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: An activity (task/email) associated with multiple contacts in HubSpot tries to sync to Salesforce. Salesforce only supports one contact association per activity.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Configure the integration to sync only the primary association. Or create separate activity records per contact in Salesforce.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: HubSpot allows duplicate contacts by email; Salesforce enforces one Contact per unique email. Different record creation logic in each system.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Use a unique identifier (e.g., Salesforce ID stored in HubSpot as custom property) to match records. Define system of record for each object type. Use deduplication tools on both sides.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Integration user lacks Read/Edit on objects or fields being synced. Or user profile doesn't have API Enabled permission.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Create a dedicated integration user with the HubSpot Integration Permission Set. Verify Read/Edit on all objects (Contact, Lead, Opportunity, Account, Task, Event) and their fields.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: A Salesforce Validation Rule fires when HubSpot updates a record, rejecting the change.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Add a formula condition to Salesforce validation rules to skip the integration user: e.g., $User.Email != 'integration@company.com'. Audit all validation rules that might affect synced objects.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Salesforce automations (triggers, flows, workflows) modify or reject data during the sync process.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Disable or modify Salesforce automations that fire on the integration user's updates. Use a before-save flow to prevent interference.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: HubSpot integration consumes too many Salesforce API calls, exceeding the daily allocation (e.g., 15K for Developer, 100K+user for Enterprise).
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Set a lower API call allocation in HubSpot integration settings. Monitor via /services/data/v66.0/limits. Use Salesforce Composite API to batch calls. Consider purchasing additional API call add-ons.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: HubSpot suspends the integration for various reasons: API limits exceeded, permission errors, org locked, storage limits exceeded.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Check HubSpot integration error panel. Resolve underlying issue. Click 'Clear Suspension'. Common causes: API limit exceeded (60% of enterprise issues), Object permissions missing, Field permissions missing.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: HubSpot tries to assign a Salesforce task to a Queue owner, which is not supported for task assignment.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Configure the integration to assign tasks to individual users, not queues. Remove queue-based assignment rules in Salesforce.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Salesforce opportunities synced with a missing Record Type. Occurs when Record Types are enabled in Salesforce but some opportunities don't have one assigned.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Either assign a Record Type to all opportunities, or deactivate Record Types for the Opportunity object, or create a default Record Type.

community_solutions.problem: Sync delays — integration runs every 15 minutes, not real-time
community_solutions.solution: Use custom middleware (Zapier, n8n, Workato) for near-real-time sync. Or use HubSpot Operations Hub for more frequent sync intervals.

community_solutions.problem: "Create and associate companies" in HubSpot creates duplicate Companies in Salesforce
community_solutions.solution: Disable 'Create and associate companies' in HubSpot Salesforce integration settings. Map company/account fields carefully to avoid auto-creation of duplicates.

community_solutions.problem: Field history tracking needed for debugging sync issues
community_solutions.solution: Enable Field History Tracking in Salesforce for key fields. Use HubSpot Sync Activity tab on contact/company records for record-level sync insights.
